title: Gonolobus volubilis
taxonomy: Gonolobus volubilis is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to the Americas and is a member of the genus Gonolobus, which is a group of twining vines. Gonolobus volubilis is further classified as belonging to the order Gentianales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The classification of Gonolobus volubilis at the subfamily level is Ascleptoideae, a group of plants characterized by their twining habit and trumpet-shaped flowers. At the tribe level, Gonolobus volubilis is classified as belonging to the tribe Asclepieae.

The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may contribute to its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. Some studies suggest that Gonolobus volubilis may also have antiviral properties, potentially making it useful in the treatment of viral infections.
